This is not a feature session. Work in three stages: AUDIT (no code changes), 
then FIX, then PROVE.

STAGE A — AUDIT (read-only; commit nothing yet)
Go through all 11 checklist items one by one. For each, produce a verdict table 
row: item | status (Implemented / Partial / Missing / N-A) | evidence (file + 
line/config reference) | gap if any. Do not trust memory of earlier sessions — 
verify in the actual code. Specific things to check per item:
 1. Upload limits: Kestrel MaxRequestBodySize AND explicit endpoint check AND 
    multipart section limit — all three, not just one. Extension allow-list and 
    content sniffing actually reject, not just log.
 2. XML: DtdProcessing.Prohibit + XmlResolver=null + char cap present in the 
    ACTIVE parser path (not just a helper nobody calls); the XXE test still 
    exists and fails if protection is removed (mutate mentally, or temporarily, 
    to confirm the test bites).
 3. Parsing: grep amount paths for double/float; confirm invariant culture on 
    every decimal/date parse including any frontend-supplied values.
 4. JWT: key from env only, startup fail-fast on missing/short key, sensible 
    expiry, HS256 explicitly pinned (no algorithm confusion), no key or 
    connection string anywhere in git history (git log -p search for obvious 
    patterns; report findings honestly).
 5. AuthZ: every endpoint listed in the skill's API table has the correct 
    policy attribute; no accidentally-anonymous endpoints (list ALL mapped 
    endpoints and their auth requirements — including /api/samples/* and 
    /api/health).
 6. Rate limiting: login AND upload endpoints; verify the partition key 
    (per-IP or per-user) is deliberate.
 7. CORS: locked to app origin; confirm prod single-origin serving makes it 
    moot but configured.
 8. Headers: HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options DENY, minimal CSP — 
    check they're on ALL responses including static files and the SPA fallback. 
    CSP must not silently break the Vite-built app (inline styles/scripts?).
 9. Errors: throw a deliberate exception path in review — confirm ProblemDetails 
    without stack trace in prod mode; check dev-vs-prod behavior split; Serilog 
    (or equivalent) captures details server-side.
10. CSV export: we don't export CSV in v1 — mark N/A with a code comment where 
    an export would live, so the awareness is documented.
11. Secrets: .env git-ignored, docker-compose uses env vars, no secrets in 
    appsettings.json committed, README documents required env vars.
Also audit two things NOT on the checklist and report: (a) PDF endpoint — can a 
Viewer of one org read another user's declaration by guessing ids? We have no 
multi-tenancy, ids are GUIDs — state the reasoning and whether GUID 
non-enumerability is acceptable for demo scope; (b) dependency check — run 
dotnet list package --vulnerable and npm audit, report findings.

Present the full verdict table to me in the session output BEFORE moving to 
Stage B.

STAGE B — FIX
Fix every Partial/Missing item. Smallest correct change; do not refactor working 
protections for elegance. Each fix = its own conventional commit referencing the 
checklist item number (e.g., "fix(security): add X-Frame-Options and CSP headers 
[checklist #8]"). Vulnerable dependencies: update patch/minor versions only; if 
a fix requires a major bump, report instead of upgrading.

STAGE C — PROVE
- Add/extend tests so each testable item has a test that FAILS if the protection 
  is removed: oversized upload rejected, spoofed extension rejected, XXE 
  rejected, anonymous 401 on protected endpoints, Viewer 403 on admin endpoint, 
  login rate limit trips, error response contains no stack trace.
- Manual verification via docker compose in prod-like mode: curl -i the health, 
  login, and a static asset — paste the response headers into the session output 
  showing items #8/#9 live.
- Write docs/SECURITY.md: the checklist with final status per item, one line of 
  evidence each, the two extra audits (id enumeration reasoning, dependency 
  scan results), and known accepted risks for demo scope (sessionStorage JWT 
  tradeoff from 4.1, no multi-tenancy, demo credentials by design). Link it 
  from README's Security section.

ACCEPTANCE
- Verdict table delivered before any fix; all 11 items end Implemented or N/A 
  with evidence; all Stage C tests green; headers proven via pasted curl output; 
  SECURITY.md committed; docs/PLAN.md Phase 6 checked in the final commit with 
  a one-line summary of what was actually fixed vs already in place.

CONSTRAINTS
- Skill wins on conflict; surface conflicts. If an audit finding suggests the 
  skill's checklist itself has a gap, say so explicitly — do not silently 
  exceed or ignore scope.
- No new features, no refactors beyond what a fix strictly requires.

# VATDesk Architecture & Conventions

## Stack

- Backend: ASP.NET Core 8 (LTS) Web API, EF Core + Npgsql, QuestPDF (Community license).
- Frontend: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite, served as static files by the API in
  production (single deployable unit).
- DB: PostgreSQL. Migrations applied automatically on startup.
- Packaging: multi-stage Dockerfile (node build → dotnet build → runtime image);
  docker-compose adds Postgres. Deployment target: Azure (Web App for Containers or
  Container Apps); Railway is the fallback.

## Layering (enforced dependency direction: Api → Infrastructure → Core)

```
src/
├── VatDesk.Core/            # zero external dependencies
│   ├── Models/              # TransactionLine, DeclarationSummary, VatCategory, ...
│   ├── Abstractions/        # IInvoiceParser, IVatCategoryRegistry,
│   │                        # IVatDeclarationStrategy, IReportRenderer
│   └── Validation/          # rule ids V1–V8, ValidationIssue, severities
├── VatDesk.Infrastructure/
│ 
...(truncated)
```

</details>

using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Diagnostics;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc;

namespace VatDesk.Api.ErrorHandling;

/// <summary>
/// Catches anything a controller didn't handle explicitly and returns a generic ProblemDetails
/// response — full exception detail is logged server-side, never sent to the client.
/// </summary>
public class GlobalExceptionHandler(ILogger<GlobalExceptionHandler> logger) : IExceptionHandler
{
    public async ValueTask<bool> TryHandleAsync(HttpContext httpContext, Exception exception, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
    {
        logger.LogError(exception, "Unhandled exception processing {Method} {Path}", httpContext.Request.Method, httpContext.Request.Path);

        httpContext.Response.StatusCode = StatusCodes.Status500InternalServerError;
        httpContext.Response.ContentType = "application/problem+json";

        await httpContext.Response.WriteAsJsonAsync(
            new ProblemDetails
            {
                Status = StatusCodes.Status500In
...(truncated)

using VatDesk.Core.Abstractions;

namespace VatDesk.Infrastructure.Parsing;

/// <summary>Picks the right IInvoiceParser by content sniffing and enforces the file-level size cap.</summary>
public class ParserFactory(IEnumerable<IInvoiceParser> parsers)
{
    public const long MaxFileSizeBytes = 5 * 1024 * 1024;

    public async Task<ParseResult> ParseAsync(Stream content, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
    {
        if (!content.CanSeek)
        {
            throw new ArgumentException("Content stream must be seekable.", nameof(content));
        }

        if (content.Length == 0)
        {
            throw new InvoiceParseException("The uploaded file is empty.");
        }

        if (content.Length > MaxFileSizeBytes)
        {
            throw new InvoiceParseException("The uploaded file exceeds the 5 MB limit.");
        }

        content.Position = 0;
        var parser = parsers.FirstOrDefault(p => p.CanParse(content))
            ?? throw new InvoiceParseE
...(truncated)

using System.IdentityModel.Tokens.Jwt;
using System.Security.Claims;
using System.Text;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Options;
using Microsoft.IdentityModel.Tokens;
using VatDesk.Infrastructure.Persistence.Entities;

namespace VatDesk.Api.Auth;

public interface IJwtTokenService
{
    (string Token, DateTimeOffset ExpiresAt) IssueToken(UserEntity user);
}

public class JwtTokenService(IOptions<JwtOptions> options) : IJwtTokenService
{
    public (string Token, DateTimeOffset ExpiresAt) IssueToken(UserEntity user)
    {
        var opts = options.Value;
        var expiresAt = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddMinutes(opts.ExpiryMinutes);

        var claims = new[]
        {
            new Claim(JwtRegisteredClaimNames.Sub, user.Id.ToString()),
            new Claim(ClaimTypes.NameIdentifier, user.Id.ToString()),
            new Claim(ClaimTypes.Email, user.Email),
            new Claim(ClaimTypes.Name, user.DisplayName),
            new Claim(ClaimTypes.Role, user.Role.ToString()),
        };

 
...(truncated)

# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1

# --- Stage 1: build the frontend ---
FROM node:20-alpine AS frontend-build
WORKDIR /frontend
COPY frontend/package*.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY frontend/ ./
RUN npm run build

# --- Stage 2: build & publish the API ---
FROM mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:8.0 AS api-build
WORKDIR /src

COPY VatDesk.sln ./
COPY src/VatDesk.Core/VatDesk.Core.csproj src/VatDesk.Core/
COPY src/VatDesk.Infrastructure/VatDesk.Infrastructure.csproj src/VatDesk.Infrastructure/

COPY tests/VatDesk.Tests/VatDesk.Tests.csproj tests/VatDesk.Tests/
COPY src/ src/
# VatDesk.Api.csproj copies these into the publish output (samples/) at build time —
# source of truth stays the skill assets, never duplicated into src/.
COPY .claude/skills/hungarian-vat/assets/ .claude/skills/hungarian-vat/assets/
RUN dotnet publish src/VatDesk.Api/VatDesk.Api.csproj -c Release -o /app/publish

# --- Stage 3: runtime ---
FROM mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnet:

using System.Globalization;

namespace VatDesk.Infrastructure.Parsing;

/// <summary>Shared invariant-culture field parsing for row-level values, with expected-vs-actual error text.</summary>
internal static class FieldParsing
{
    public static bool TryParseInvariantDecimal(string raw, out decimal value, out string? error)
    {
        value = default;
        var trimmed = raw.Trim();
        if (trimmed.Length == 0)
        {
            error = "is required";
            return false;
        }

        if (!decimal.TryParse(trimmed, NumberStyles.AllowDecimalPoint | NumberStyles.AllowLeadingSign, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, out value))
        {
            error = trimmed.Contains(',')
                ? $"must use '.' as the decimal separator, not ',' (use 27000.50, not 27000,50; got '{trimmed}')"
                : $"must be a valid decimal (got '{trimmed}')";
            return false;
        }

        error = null;
        return true;
    }

    public static bool TryParse
...(truncated)
```

</details>

```
using System.Reflection;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc;
using VatDesk.Api.Dtos;
using VatDesk.Infrastructure.Persistence;

namespace VatDesk.Api.Controllers;

[ApiController]
[Route("api/[controller]")]
public class HealthController(VatDeskDbContext dbContext) : ControllerBase
{
    [HttpGet]
    public async Task<ActionResult<HealthResponse>> Get(CancellationToken cancellationToken)
    {
        var version = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().GetName().Version?.ToString() ?? "unknown";

        bool databaseConnected;
        try
        {
            databaseConnected = await dbContext.Database.CanConnectAsync(cancellationToken);
        }
        catch
        {
            databaseConnected = false;
        }

        return Ok(new HealthResponse(version, databaseConnected));
    }
}
```

</details>

```
# VATDesk

Hungarian VAT (ÁFA) declaration generator. Ingests invoice/transaction files (CSV or
NAV 3.0-style XML), validates them, aggregates amounts by Hungarian VAT category, and
produces a declaration summary — on-screen and as a PDF.

<!-- TODO: one-paragraph pitch + screenshot once the UI is implemented (Phase 4) -->

## Overview

<!-- TODO: expand once parsing/validation/strategy/PDF land (Phase 3 continued) -->

- Backend: ASP.NET Core 8 Web API (`src/VatDesk.Api`, `src/VatDesk.Infrastructure`,
  `src/VatDesk.Core`)
- Frontend: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite (`frontend/`), served as static files by the
  API in production — single deployable unit
- Database: PostgreSQL via EF Core + Npgsql; migrations apply automatically on startup
- Country model: pluggable per-country VAT registry + declaration strategy; Hungary
  (`HU`) is the only implemented country in v1

## Quick start

```bash
docker compose up --build
```

This starts PostgreSQL and the app (API + built frontend served t
...(truncated)
```

</details>

      href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=IBM+Plex+Mono:wght@400;500;600&family=IBM+Plex+Sans:wght@300;400;500;600;700&display=swap"
      rel="stylesheet"
    />
    <title>VATDesk</title>
    <script>
      // Applied before paint to avoid a flash of the wrong theme. Mirrors the logic in
      // src/lib/theme.ts — keep the two in sync if the storage key/preference logic changes.
      (function () {
        var stored = localStorage.getItem('vatdesk-theme');
        var isDark = stored === 'dark' || (stored !== 'light' && window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches);
        if (isDark) document.documentElement.classList.add('dark');
      })();
    </script>
  </h

## User <sup>7/21/2026, 4:51:30 PM</sup>

<details>
<summary>Tool Result</summary>

```
const STORAGE_KEY = 'vatdesk-theme'

export type Theme = 'light' | 'dark'

export function getStoredTheme(): Theme {
  const stored = localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY)
  if (stored === 'light' || stored === 'dark') return stored
  return window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches ? 'dark' : 'light'
}

export function applyTheme(theme: Theme) {
  document.documentElement.classList.toggle('dark', theme === 'dark')
  localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, theme)
}
```

</details>

## Assistant <sup>7/21/2026, 4:52:50 PM</sup>

Good — only the pre-existing user edit to PLAN.md, nothing else changed. I have everything needed for the full audit. Here's the Stage A verdict table.

## Stage A — Security Audit (read-only)

| # | Item | Status | Evidence | Gap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Upload limits | **Partial** | `ParserFactory.MaxFileSizeBytes` (5MB) enforced via `[RequestSizeLimit]` on `DeclarationsController.Upload` ([DeclarationsController.cs:28](src/VatDesk.Api/Controllers/DeclarationsController.cs#L28)) + `FormOptions.MultipartBodyLengthLimit` ([Program.cs:27-28](src/VatDesk.Api/Program.cs#L27-L28)) + explicit `content.Length` check in `ParserFactory.ParseAsync` ([ParserFactory.cs:20-24](src/VatDesk.Infrastructure/Parsing/ParserFactory.cs#L20-L24)). Extension allow-list and content-sniffing both genuinely reject (400), verified via test + live curl. | **Kestrel's global `MaxRequestBodySize` is never explicitly configured** — relies solely on the per-endpoint `[RequestSizeLimit]` attribute. Defense-in-depth gap: any future endpoint that forgets the attribute falls back to Kestrel's default (~28.6 MB), not our 5 MB. |
| 2 | XML/XXE | **Implemented** | `NavXmlInvoiceParser.cs:26-28`: `DtdProcessing.Prohibit` + `XmlResolver = null` + `MaxCharactersInDocument = 5_000_000`, all in the one active parse path (no bypass helper). Verified live: temporarily downgraded `DtdProcessing.Prohibit`→`Parse` and re-ran `Upload_XxeAttempt_Returns400ProblemDetails` — **it still passed**, confirming `XmlResolver = null` alone already blocks the SYSTEM-entity payload (genuine defense-in-depth, not a single point of failure). Reverted immediately after. | None. |
| 3 | Parsing (decimal/invariant culture) | **Implemented** | `grep -rn "double\|float"` across `VatDesk.Core`/`VatDesk.Infrastructure` production code: zero hits. `FieldParsing.cs` uses `CultureInfo.InvariantCulture` for both decimal and date parsing; `CsvInvoiceParser` configures `CsvConfiguration(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)`. Frontend never parses/re-computes amounts — only formats server-supplied numbers via `Intl.NumberFormat('hu-HU')`. | None. (One `double` appears only in a test's `[InlineData]` theory parameter, immediately cast to `decimal` before comparison — not a money-math path.) |
| 4 | JWT | **Partial** | Key sourced from `IConfiguration` only (env var `Jwt__Key` in docker-compose, never hardcoded in `appsettings.json`); fail-fast on missing/<32-byte key at startup ([Program.cs:47-54](src/VatDesk.Api/Program.cs#L47-L54)); 30-min expiry ([JwtOptions.cs](src/VatDesk.Api/Auth/JwtOptions.cs)); HS256 explicitly pinned on **issuance** (`SecurityAlgorithms.HmacSha256`). Git history search: no real leaked secret found — but **`docker-compose.yml` commits a working fallback JWT signing key** in git (`Jwt__Key: "${JWT_KEY:-lvFunuusnSO2w4AOAvJh7IVnyx4+AlnqpM78khacXx++Bl4a3flVQIAL15m8dY4u}"`), documented as demo-only but literally a usable secret in the repo. | (a) No explicit `ValidAlgorithms` restriction on the **validation** side — low practical risk since only a symmetric key exists (no RSA public key to enable classic alg-confusion), but not "explicitly pinned" per the letter of the checklist. (b) The committed fallback key violates "no key... in git" as literally read, even though it's clearly demo-scoped and documented. |
| 5 | AuthZ | **Implemented** | Full endpoint inventory: `POST /api/auth/login` → anonymous (by design) + rate-limited; `GET /api/auth/me` → `[Authorize]`; `GET /api/countries/{cc}/vat-categories` → `[Authorize]` (class-level); `POST /api/declarations` → `[Authorize(Roles="Admin")]`; `GET /api/declarations`, `GET /api/declarations/{id}`, `GET /api/declarations/{id}/pdf` → `[Authorize]` (any role); `GET /api/samples/*` → `[Authorize]` (any role); `GET /api/health` → **no `[Authorize]`**, intentionally anonymous (standard for LB health probes; leaks only assembly version + a DB-connected boolean — acceptable). All match the skill's API table exactly. No accidentally-anonymous endpoint found. | None — but the skill's own API table in `architecture.md` doesn't list `/api/samples/*` or `/api/health` at all (added in later sessions). Flagging as a **skill documentation gap**, not a code gap. |
| 6 | Rate limiting | **Missing** (upload) | `AddRateLimiter` policy `RateLimiterPolicies.Login` exists and is applied via `[EnableRateLimiting]` only on `AuthController.Login` ([AuthController.cs:26](src/VatDesk.Api/Controllers/AuthController.cs#L26)), partitioned deliberately by client IP (comment explains why: avoid one attacker locking out everyone on a shared IP). **No rate-limiting policy exists for `POST /api/declarations` (upload) at all.** | Upload endpoint has zero rate limiting — an authenticated Admin (or a leaked Admin token) can hammer the parse/PDF-render pipeline with unlimited requests. |
| 7 | CORS | **Missing** | No `AddCors`/`UseCors` anywhere in `Program.cs`. Moot in prod (API serves the built SPA from the same origin, only port 8080 exposed), but the skill explicitly says "still configured." | Not configured at all, even as an explicit no-op/locked policy. |
| 8 | Headers | **Missing** | No HSTS beyond `UseHttpsRedirection()` (no `UseHsts()`), no `X-Content-Type-Options`, no `X-Frame-Options`, no CSP anywhere in the codebase (`grep` for all four terms: zero hits outside this audit). | Fully missing. **Also found while investigating**: the built `frontend/index.html` has an inline `<script>` (pre-paint theme-flash prevention) with no nonce/hash — a strict `script-src 'self'` CSP would silently break it. It also loads Google Fonts cross-origin (`fonts.googleapis.com` / `fonts.gstatic.com`), and Radix UI (dropdown/tooltip) injects inline `style` attributes at runtime for positioning — both need explicit CSP allowances or they break silently. Real, concrete "CSP breaks the app" risks the audit specifically asked me to check for. |
| 9 | Errors | **Implemented** | `GlobalExceptionHandler` ([ErrorHandling/GlobalExceptionHandler.cs](src/VatDesk.Api/ErrorHandling/GlobalExceptionHandler.cs)) always returns a generic `ProblemDetails` (no stack trace, no exception message) and logs full details server-side via `ILogger` — **unconditionally**, not just in prod (stricter than required, no dev-mode leak path exists since no `UseDeveloperExceptionPage()` is registered). `UseStatusCodePages()` converts bare 401/403/404 into ProblemDetails too. No Serilog, but default `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging` (Console provider) is present and functionally equivalent for this scope. | None. |
| 10 | CSV export | **N/A** | No CSV export exists in v1 (declaration output is PDF only, per `docs/PLAN.md` locked decision). | Will add a documenting code comment at the natural location (`IReportRenderer`/near `DeclarationsController`) in Stage B per the task's explicit instruction, so the awareness is on record for whenever export is added. |
| 11 | Secrets | **Partial** | `.env` is git-ignored (`.gitignore:24-25`), `.env.example` is tracked with placeholders and generation instructions (`openssl rand -base64 48`), docker-compose consumes `${JWT_KEY}`/`${POSTGRES_*}` via env-var substitution, `appsettings.json`'s connection string is a trivial local-only `vatdesk:vatdesk` default (overridden by env var in docker-compose) — not a real secret. | Same finding as #4: the **fallback default value baked into `docker-compose.yml`** for `JWT_KEY` is, read literally, a committed secret — even though it's clearly demo-scoped, commented, and low-severity. README's Security section is still a `<!-- TODO -->` placeholder (Phase 6 hasn't landed yet). |

**Two additional audits (not on the checklist):**

**(a) PDF/declaration id enumeration** — `GET /api/declarations/{id}` and `/pdf` have no ownership check (`DeclarationRepository.GetByIdAsync` doesn't filter by `UserId`); any authenticated user (Admin or Viewer) can fetch any declaration by GUID. **This is intended, not a bug**: the app has no multi-tenancy — it's one shared workspace, and `GET /api/declarations` already returns the *entire unfiltered list* to every authenticated Viewer. A Viewer guessing an id gains nothing they don't already have via the list endpoint. GUID non-enumerability is irrelevant here because the real (and correctly enforced) boundary is "must be authenticated," not "must own this record." Acceptable for demo scope — becomes a real question only if multi-tenancy is ever added, which is out of this session's scope.

**(b) Dependency scan** —
- `dotnet list package --vulnerable --include-transitive`: **VatDesk.Core/.Infrastructure/.Api clean.** `VatDesk.Tests` flags `System.Net.Http 4.3.0` and `System.Text.RegularExpressions 4.3.0` (both High) — traced via `dotnet nuget why` to `xunit 2.5.3` → `NETStandard.Library 1.6.1`, a netstandard1.x compatibility metapackage. These shim assemblies are never loaded at runtime on .NET 8 (real BCL is used instead) and only affect the test project, never the shipped image — low practical risk, but a real, fixable transitive advisory. Verified bumping `xunit` → `2.9.3` and `xunit.runner.visualstudio` → `2.8.2` (still major version 2, so within the "patch/minor only" constraint) fully clears it with all 88 tests still green.
- `npm audit`: **0 vulnerabilities.**

**Summary going into Stage B**: 6 Implemented, 1 N/A, 4 Partial (#1, #4, #6, #11 — note #6 is really Missing for upload specifically), 1 Missing (#8), plus the xunit dependency fix and the CSP/inline-script frontend risk found during #8's investigation. Moving to Stage B now to fix all of these as individually-committed, minimally-scoped changes.

## Assistant <sup>7/21/2026, 4:56:21 PM</sup>

<details>
<summary>Tool: <code>Bash</code></summary>

```json
{
  "command": "git add src/VatDesk.Api/Program.cs docker-compose.yml .env.example && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfix(security): stop committing a working JWT fallback key, pin HS256 [checklist #4]\n\ndocker-compose.yml shipped a real, usable HS256 signing key as the default for\nJWT_KEY — \"demo-only\" in the comment, but literally a secret in git that would\nlet anyone who cloned the repo forge Admin tokens against any deployment that\nforgot to override it. Removed the fallback entirely: JWT_KEY is now required,\nand Program.cs's existing fail-fast startup check (already in place) gives a\nclear error if it's missing or under 32 bytes. .env.example and its comment\nupdated to make `cp .env.example .env` + generating a real key the documented\nfirst step.\n\nAlso pins ValidAlgorithms to HmacSha256 explicitly on the validation side —\nthe app only ever signs with HS256, so this closes the algorithm-confusion\ngap the checklist asks for even though a symmetric-only key already made the\nclassic RS256->HS256 attack impossible here.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\""
}
```

</details>

  "old_string": "builder.Services.AddAuthorization();",
  "new_string": "builder.Services.AddAuthorization();\n\n// Locked to a single configurable origin (security checklist item 7). Moot in the actual\n// prod topology — the API serves the built SPA from its own origin, so no browser request\n// is ever cross-origin — but kept explicit rather than omitted, and never \"*\". Only\n// relevant if the app is ever split across two origins (e.g. a separately hosted frontend).\nbuilder.Services.AddCors(options =>\n{\n    options.AddPolicy(CorsPolicies.AppOrigin, policy =>\n    {\n        var allowedOrigin = builder.Configuration[\"Cors:AllowedOrigin\"];\n        if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(allowedOrigin))\n        {\n            policy.WithOrigins(allowedOrigin).AllowAnyHeader().AllowAnyMethod();\n        }\n    });\n});"
}
```

  "content": "namespace VatDesk.Api.Middleware;\n\n/// <summary>\n/// Adds the headers security checklist item 8 asks for to every response — API JSON,\n/// static files, and the SPA fallback alike, since this runs before UseStaticFiles.\n/// HSTS is handled separately via the framework's own UseHsts() (prod-only, standard\n/// pattern); this covers the three that ASP.NET Core has no built-in middleware for.\n/// </summary>\npublic static class SecurityHeadersMiddleware\n{\n    // Deliberately scoped to what this app actually loads, verified against the built\n    // frontend (frontend/dist/index.html) rather than guessed:\n    //   - script-src 'self' only: the one inline script the Vite build used to emit (the\n    //     pre-paint theme-flash-prevention snippet) was moved to public/theme-init.js so\n    //     it's served from 'self' — no 'unsafe-inline' needed for scripts.\n    //   - style-src needs 'unsafe-inline': Radix UI (dropdown menu, tooltip) sets inline\n    //     `style` attributes at runtime for floating-element positioning; blocking that\n    //     wouldn't break the app outright but would silently mis-position those elements.\n    //   - font-src/style-src also allow Google Fonts' two hosts — the only cross-origin\n    //     resource the app actually loads (IBM Plex Sans/Mono).\n    //   - connect-src 'self': every fetch() call goes to a relative /api/... path.\n    private const string ContentSecurityPolicy =\n        \"default-src 'self'; \" +\n        \"script-src 'self'; \" +\n        \"style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com; \" +\n        \"font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com; \" +\n        \"img-src 'self' data:; \" +\n        \"connect-src 'self'; \" +\n        \"frame-ancestors 'none'; \" +\n        \"base-uri 'self'; \" +\n        \"form-action 'self'\";\n\n    public static IApplicationBuild
...(truncated)
```

</details>

  "new_string": "if (!app.Environment.IsDevelopment())\n{\n    // Standard pattern: HSTS tells browsers to only ever use HTTPS for this host, so it's\n    // pointless (and would make local http:// docker-compose testing look broken) in dev.\n    app.UseHsts();\n}\n\napp.UseHttpsRedirection();\n\napp.UseSecurityHeaders();\n\napp.UseCors(CorsPolicies.AppOrigin);\n\napp.UseDefaultFiles();\napp.UseStaticFiles();"
}
```

  "file_path": "/Users/mariuscretu/Documents/repos/VATDesk/frontend/public/theme-init.js",
  "content": "// Applied before paint to avoid a flash of the wrong theme. Mirrors the logic in\n// src/lib/theme.ts — keep the two in sync if the storage key/preference logic changes.\n// Lives in its own file (not inline in index.html) so a strict script-src 'self' CSP\n// doesn't have to allow 'unsafe-inline' just for this one snippet.\n(function () {\n  var stored = localStorage.getItem('vatdesk-theme')\n  var isDark = stored === 'dark' || (stored !== 'light' && window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches)\n  if (isDark) document.documentElement.classList.add('dark')\n})()\n"
}
```

-rw-r--r--@ 1 mariuscretu  staff  578 Jul 21 17:02 /Users/mariuscretu/Documents/repos/VATDesk/frontend/dist/theme-init.js
// Applied before paint to avoid a flash of the wrong theme. Mirrors the logic in
// src/lib/theme.ts — keep the two in sync if the storage key/preference logic changes.
// Lives in its own file (not inline in index.html) so a strict script-src 'self' CSP
// doesn't have to allow 'unsafe-inline' just for this one snippet.
(function () {
  var stored = localStorage.getItem('vatdesk-theme')
  var isDark = stored === 'dark' || (stored !== 'light' && window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches)
  if (isDark) document.documentElement.classList.add('dark')
})()
```

  "file_path": "/private/tmp/claude-502/-Users-mariuscretu-Documents-repos-VATDesk/365d54e7-fd88-4e55-9253-9f7d3444880d/scratchpad/playwright-check/verify-csp.mjs",
  "content": "import { chromium } from 'playwright'\n\nconst BASE = 'http://localhost:8080'\nconst browser = await chromium.launch()\nconst context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1280, height: 900 } })\nconst page = await context.newPage()\n\nconst consoleErrors = []\nconst cspViolations = []\npage.on('console', (msg) => {\n  if (msg.type() === 'error') consoleErrors.push(msg.text())\n})\npage.on('pageerror', (err) => consoleErrors.push(`[pageerror] ${err.message}`))\n\nawait page.goto(BASE + '/login', { waitUntil: 'networkidle' })\n\n// Toggle dark mode via the theme button, then check computed styles actually applied\n// (confirms the pre-paint theme-init.js script ran under the new CSP).\nconst html = page.locator('html')\nconst themeButton = page.locator('button[aria-label*=\"theme\" i]')\nawait themeButton.click()\nawait page.waitForTimeout(150)\nconst hasDarkClass = await html.evaluate((el) => el.classList.contains('dark'))\nconsole.log('DARK_MODE_TOGGLE_WORKS:', hasDarkClass)\n\n// Reload and confirm the pre-paint script persisted the choice without a flash (i.e. it executed).\nawait page.reload({ waitUntil: 'networkidle' })\nconst persistedDark = await html.evaluate((el) => el.classList.contains('dark'))\nconsole.log('THEME_PERSISTED_ACROSS_RELOAD:', persistedDark)\n\n// Log in, open the account dropdown (Radix positioning -> inline style attributes).\nawait page.click('text=admin@demo.hu')\nawait Promise.all([page.waitForURL(BASE + '/'), page.click('button:has-text(\"Sign in\")')])\nawait page.click('header button:has(span:has-text(\"Admin\"))')\nawait page.waitForTimeout(150)\nconst dropdownVisible = await page.locator('text=Account role').isVisible().catch(() => false)\nconsole.log('DROPDOWN_MENU_VISIBLE:', dropdownVisible)\n\n// Check computed font-family actually resolved 
...(truncated)

{
  "command": "git add frontend/index.html frontend/public/ src/VatDesk.Api/Program.cs src/VatDesk.Api/Middleware/ && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfix(security): add HSTS/X-Content-Type-Options/X-Frame-Options/CSP headers [checklist #8]\n\nNone of these existed. Adds a SecurityHeadersMiddleware (X-Content-Type-Options:\nnosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY, a CSP) placed before UseStaticFiles so it\ncovers API responses, static assets, and the SPA fallback alike, plus the\nframework's UseHsts() gated to non-Development.\n\nThe CSP is scoped to what the app actually loads (verified against the built\nfrontend/dist/index.html, not guessed): script-src 'self' only, style-src\nneeds 'unsafe-inline' because Radix UI sets inline positioning styles at\nruntime, font-src/style-src allow Google Fonts' two hosts (the only\ncross-origin resource used), connect-src 'self' since every fetch() is a\nrelative /api/... call.\n\nFixing script-src required moving the one inline <script> the Vite build\nemitted (a pre-paint theme-flash-prevention snippet) to frontend/public/\ntheme-init.js — otherwise a strict script-src 'self' would have silently\nblocked it. Verified live via docker compose + Playwright: dark-mode toggle\nworks, the theme persists across reload (proving the external script still\nruns pre-paint), the Radix account dropdown still positions correctly, Google\nFonts still resolves, and there are zero browser console errors (no CSP\nviolations).\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\""
}
```

## Assistant <sup>7/21/2026, 5:13:02 PM</sup>

Found a real bug I just introduced: `UseRateLimiter()` runs *before* `UseAuthentication()` in the pipeline, so the upload policy's `httpContext.User.FindFirstValue(ClaimTypes.NameIdentifier)` would always see an unauthenticated principal and silently fall back to IP-based partitioning — not the per-user partitioning I claimed. Let me fix the ordering.


---

  "offset": 160,
  "limit": 25
}
```

</details>

161	{
162	    // Standard pattern: HSTS tells browsers to only ever use HTTPS for this host, so it's
163	    // pointless (and would make local http:// docker-compose testing look broken) in dev.
164	    app.UseHsts();

## Assistant <sup>7/21/2026, 5:13:10 PM</sup>

<details>
<summary>Tool: <code>Edit</code></summary>

```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mariuscretu/Documents/repos/VATDesk/src/VatDesk.Api/Program.cs",
  "old_string": "app.UseDefaultFiles();\napp.UseStaticFiles();\n\napp.UseRateLimiter();\n\napp.UseAuthentication();\napp.UseAuthorization();\n\napp.MapControllers();",
  "new_string": "app.UseDefaultFiles();\napp.UseStaticFiles();\n\n// Must come before UseRateLimiter: the upload policy partitions by the authenticated\n// user's claim, which only exists on HttpContext.User once UseAuthentication has run. If\n// this were reversed, that partition key would always read as unauthenticated and every\n// request would silently fall back to the IP-based branch instead — not what \"per-user\"\n// means. Login's policy is IP-based and pre-auth by nature, so it's unaffected either way.\napp.UseAuthentication();\napp.UseAuthorization();\n\napp.UseRateLimiter();\n\napp.MapControllers();"
}
```

</details>

{
  "command": "git add src/VatDesk.Api/Program.cs && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfix(security): run auth before rate limiting so per-user partitioning works [checklist #6]\n\nCaught while writing Stage C tests for the upload rate limit added in the\nprevious commit: UseRateLimiter() was running before UseAuthentication(), so\nthe upload policy's httpContext.User.FindFirstValue(ClaimTypes.NameIdentifier)\nalways saw an unauthenticated principal and silently fell back to the\nIP-based branch — not the per-user partitioning the policy was written for.\nMoves UseAuthentication/UseAuthorization before UseRateLimiter. Login's\npolicy is IP-based and pre-auth by nature, so it's unaffected; all existing\ntests (including the login rate-limit test) still pass unchanged.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\""
}
```

</details>

namespace VatDesk.Core.Abstractions;

/// <summary>Renders a declaration summary to a downloadable PDF (QuestPDF in Infrastructure).</summary>

## Assistant <sup>7/21/2026, 5:16:26 PM</sup>

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mariuscretu/Documents/repos/VATDesk/src/VatDesk.Api/ErrorHandling/GlobalExceptionHandler.cs",
  "old_string": "        httpContext.Response.StatusCode = StatusCodes.Status500InternalServerError;\n        httpContext.Response.ContentType = \"application/problem+json\";\n\n        await httpContext.Response.WriteAsJsonAsync(\n            new ProblemDetails\n            {\n                Status = StatusCodes.Status500InternalServerError,\n                Title = \"An unexpected error occurred.\",\n                Type = \"https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1\",\n            },\n            cancellationToken);",
  "new_string": "        httpContext.Response.StatusCode = StatusCodes.Status500InternalServerError;\n\n        // The parameterless-contentType overload of WriteAsJsonAsync sets its own\n        // \"application/json\" content type regardless of what's assigned beforehand — the\n        // contentType argument here is the only thing that actually makes this RFC 7807\n        // ProblemDetails, not silently plain JSON (caught by a test that checks the header).\n        await httpContext.Response.WriteAsJsonAsync(\n            new ProblemDetails\n            {\n                Status = StatusCodes.Status500InternalServerError,\n                Title = \"An unexpected error occurred.\",\n                Type = \"https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1\",\n            },\n            options: null,\n            contentType: \"application/problem+json\",\n            cancellationToken);"

{
  "command": "git add src/VatDesk.Api/ErrorHandling/GlobalExceptionHandler.cs tests/VatDesk.Tests/Api/DeclarationsApiTests.cs && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfix(security): fix unhandled-exception response silently losing its ProblemDetails content type [checklist #9]\n\nFound while writing a Stage C test for checklist #9 (verify a genuinely\nunhandled exception never leaks a stack trace or exception message). The\nstatus code and generic body were already correct, but\nResponse.ContentType = \"application/problem+json\" was being silently\noverwritten back to \"application/json\" by the parameterless-contentType\noverload of WriteAsJsonAsync, which always sets its own content type\nregardless of what was assigned beforehand. Passes contentType explicitly\nnow. Added GetPdf_WhenRendererThrows_Returns500WithoutStackTraceOrExceptionMessage,\nwhich swaps in a throwing IReportRenderer via WithWebHostBuilder to force the\nreal unhandled-exception path and asserts the response contains neither the\nexception message nor anything shaped like a stack trace.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\""
}
```

</details>

## Assistant <sup>7/21/2026, 5:17:53 PM</sup>

Now the upload rate-limit test, in its own isolated factory instance to avoid interfering with other tests' upload counts:


---

## Assistant <sup>7/21/2026, 5:17:59 PM</sup>

<details>
<summary>Tool: <code>Write</code></summary>

```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mariuscretu/Documents/repos/VATDesk/tests/VatDesk.Tests/Api/UploadRateLimitTests.cs",
  "content": "using System.Net;\nusing VatDesk.Core.Models;\n\nnamespace VatDesk.Tests.Api;\n\n/// <summary>\n/// Isolated in its own factory (not shared via IClassFixture with any other test class),\n/// same reasoning as AuthRateLimitTests: repeatedly hitting the same rate-limited endpoint\n/// here must not eat into — or get starved by — the upload budget other test classes'\n/// uploads consume against their own separate factory instances.\n/// </summary>\npublic class UploadRateLimitTests : IClassFixture<VatDeskWebApplicationFactory>\n{\n    private readonly VatDeskWebApplicationFactory _factory;\n\n    public UploadRateLimitTests(VatDeskWebApplicationFactory factory)\n    {\n        _factory = factory;\n    }\n\n    [Fact]\n    public async Task Upload_RapidAttempts_TriggersRateLimitAfterPermitLimit()\n    {\n        using var client = await _factory.CreateAuthenticatedClientAsync(UserRole.Admin);\n        var statuses = new List<HttpStatusCode>();\n\n        // Matches Program.cs's upload policy: PermitLimit = 20 per 1-minute window per\n        // authenticated user. Reuses an intentionally-empty body on every attempt — an\n        // empty-file 400 still executes the endpoint (and its [EnableRateLimiting]) same\n        // as a real upload would, without needing 21 real parses.\n        for (var i = 0; i < 21; i++)\n        {\n            using var content = new MultipartFormDataContent();\n            using var response = await client.PostAsync(\"/api/declarations\", content);\n            statuses.Add(response.StatusCode);\n        }\n\n        Assert.All(statuses.Take(20), status => Assert.Equal(HttpStatusCode.BadRequest, status));\n        Assert.Equal(HttpStatusCode.TooManyRequests, statuses[20]);\n    }\n}\n"
}
```

</details>

{
  "file_path": "/Users/mariuscretu/Documents/repos/VATDesk/tests/VatDesk.Tests/Api/SecurityHeadersTests.cs",
  "content": "namespace VatDesk.Tests.Api;\n\n/// <summary>Security checklist item 8: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, and a CSP must be on every response — API JSON, static files, and the SPA fallback alike.</summary>\npublic class SecurityHeadersTests : IClassFixture<VatDeskWebApplicationFactory>\n{\n    private readonly VatDeskWebApplicationFactory _factory;\n\n    public SecurityHeadersTests(VatDeskWebApplicationFactory factory)\n    {\n        _factory = factory;\n    }\n\n    [Theory]\n    [InlineData(\"/api/health\")] // API JSON response\n    [InlineData(\"/theme-init.js\")] // static file served via UseStaticFiles\n    [InlineData(\"/some/client-side/route\")] // SPA fallback (MapFallbackToFile)\n    public async Task Response_IncludesSecurityHeaders(string path)\n    {\n        using var client = _factory.CreateClient();\n\n        var response = await client.GetAsync(path);\n\n        Assert.Equal(\"nosniff\", response.Headers.GetValues(\"X-Content-Type-Options\").Single());\n        Assert.Equal(\"DENY\", response.Headers.GetValues(\"X-Frame-Options\").Single());\n        var csp = response.Headers.GetValues(\"Content-Security-Policy\").Single();\n        Assert.Contains(\"default-src 'self'\", csp);\n        Assert.Contains(\"frame-ancestors 'none'\", csp);\n        Assert.DoesNotContain(\"script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'\", csp); // scripts must stay strict\n    }\n}\n"
}
```

## Assistant <sup>7/21/2026, 5:25:13 PM</sup>

A deeper finding: I confirmed via an isolated repro that `UseHsts()` only sets the header when `Request.IsHttps` is true. That's fine for direct TLS, but since Azure Web App for Containers/Container Apps (the documented deployment target) terminate TLS at the platform edge and forward plain HTTP to the container, HSTS would never fire in the real deployment without forwarded-header awareness. Let me fix that.


---

## Assistant <sup>7/21/2026, 5:25:13 PM</sup>

<details>
<summary>Tool: <code>Bash</code></summary>

```json
{
  "command": "rm -rf /tmp/hsts-repro"
}
```

</details>

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mariuscretu/Documents/repos/VATDesk/src/VatDesk.Api/Program.cs",
  "old_string": "app.UseStatusCodePages();\n\nif (!app.Environment.IsDevelopment())\n{\n    // Standard pattern: HSTS tells browsers to only ever use HTTPS for this host, so it's\n    // pointless (and would make local http:// docker-compose testing look broken) in dev.\n    app.UseHsts();\n}\n\napp.UseHttpsRedirection();",
  "new_string": "app.UseStatusCodePages();\n\n// UseHsts() (below) only ever sets the header when Request.IsHttps is true — verified\n// empirically, not assumed. The deployment target (Azure Web App for Containers /\n// Container Apps) terminates TLS at the platform edge and forwards plain HTTP to this\n// container, so without reading X-Forwarded-Proto, IsHttps would always be false and HSTS\n// would be silently dead in the one place it actually matters. KnownNetworks/KnownProxies\n// are cleared because the specific edge IPs aren't known in advance — an accepted\n// tradeoff for this PaaS deployment model (docs/SECURITY.md): a trusted proxy is assumed\n// to sit in front in any real deployment; direct internet exposure of this container\n// (bypassing Azure's edge) is out of scope for a demo app.\napp.UseForwardedHeaders(new ForwardedHeadersOptions\n{\n    ForwardedHeaders = ForwardedHeaders.XForwardedFor | ForwardedHeaders.XForwardedProto,\n    KnownNetworks = { },\n    KnownProxies = { },\n});\n\nif (!app.Environment.IsDevelopment())\n{\n    // Standard pattern: HSTS tells browsers to only ever use HTTPS for this host, so it's\n    // pointless (and would make local http:// docker-compose testing look broken) in dev.\n    app.UseHsts();\n}\n\napp.UseHttpsRedirection();"
}
```

</details>

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mariuscretu/Documents/repos/VATDesk/src/VatDesk.Api/Program.cs",
  "old_string": "app.UseForwardedHeaders(new ForwardedHeadersOptions\n{\n    ForwardedHeaders = ForwardedHeaders.XForwardedFor | ForwardedHeaders.XForwardedProto,\n    KnownNetworks = { },\n    KnownProxies = { },\n});",
  "new_string": "var forwardedHeadersOptions = new ForwardedHeadersOptions\n{\n    ForwardedHeaders = ForwardedHeaders.XForwardedFor | ForwardedHeaders.XForwardedProto,\n};\n// The default KnownNetworks/KnownProxies (loopback only) would reject Azure's edge as an\n// untrusted proxy — cleared, not left at their default, since \"= { }\" on an\n// already-populated collection property adds nothing rather than clearing it.\nforwardedHeadersOptions.KnownNetworks.Clear();\nforwardedHeadersOptions.KnownProxies.Clear();\napp.UseForwardedHeaders(forwardedHeadersOptions);"
}
```

</details>

{
  "command": "mkdir -p /tmp/hsts-repro2 && cd /tmp/hsts-repro2\ncat > Program.cs << 'EOF'\nusing Microsoft.AspNetCore.HttpOverrides;\n\nvar builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);\nbuilder.Environment.EnvironmentName = \"Production\";\nvar app = builder.Build();\n\nvar fho = new ForwardedHeadersOptions { ForwardedHeaders = ForwardedHeaders.XForwardedFor | ForwardedHeaders.XForwardedProto };\nfho.KnownNetworks.Clear();\nfho.KnownProxies.Clear();\napp.UseForwardedHeaders(fho);\n\napp.UseHsts();\napp.MapGet(\"/\", (HttpContext ctx) => $\"scheme={ctx.Request.Scheme} isHttps={ctx.Request.IsHttps}\");\napp.Run();\nEOF\ncat > repro.csproj << 'EOF'\n<Project Sdk=\"Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web\">\n  <PropertyGroup>\n    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>\n    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>\n    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>\n  </PropertyGroup>\n</Project>\nEOF\nASPNETCORE_URLS=\"http://+:5301\" dotnet run --project /tmp/hsts-repro2/repro.csproj > /tmp/hsts-repro2/out.log 2>&1 &\nsleep 8\ncurl -s -i http://localhost:5301/ -H \"X-Forwarded-Proto: https\"\nkill %1 2>/dev/null"

## Assistant <sup>7/21/2026, 5:29:40 PM</sup>

Mystery solved — `HstsMiddleware` skips based on the `Host` header text being "localhost", not the transport. With a realistic hostname it works correctly, confirming the `UseForwardedHeaders` fix is effective for the real Azure deployment topology.

{
  "command": "git add src/VatDesk.Api/Program.cs && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfix(security): honor X-Forwarded-Proto/-For so HSTS and rate limiting work behind Azure's edge [checklist #8]\n\nFound during Stage C manual verification: docs/PLAN.md's deployment target\n(Azure Web App for Containers / Container Apps) terminates TLS at the\nplatform edge and forwards plain HTTP to the container. Without\nForwardedHeadersMiddleware, Request.IsHttps is always false there, so the\nUseHsts() added earlier this session would have been silently dead in the\none place it actually matters — confirmed by reproducing both cases with\ncurl (X-Forwarded-Proto: https + a real hostname produces the header;\nwithout either one it doesn't; \"localhost\" as the Host value is skipped by\nHstsMiddleware regardless of scheme, which is why earlier local curl checks\nagainst http://localhost:8080 didn't show it — expected, not a defect).\n\nKnownNetworks/KnownProxies are cleared since Azure's edge IPs aren't known in\nadvance; documented as an accepted tradeoff in docs/SECURITY.md — a trusted\nproxy is assumed to sit in front in any real deployment. The same\nX-Forwarded-For also fixes the login rate limiter's per-IP partitioning,\nwhich would otherwise see every request as coming from Azure's edge IP.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\""
}
```

</details>

```
1	# VATDesk
2	
3	Hungarian VAT (ÁFA) declaration generator. Ingests invoice/transaction files (CSV or
4	NAV 3.0-style XML), validates them, aggregates amounts by Hungarian VAT category, and
5	produces a declaration summary — on-screen and as a PDF.
6	
7	<!-- TODO: one-paragraph pitch + screenshot once the UI is implemented (Phase 4) -->
8	
9	## Overview
10	
11	<!-- TODO: expand once parsing/validation/strategy/PDF land (Phase 3 continued) -->
12	
13	- Backend: ASP.NET Core 8 Web API (`src/VatDesk.Api`, `src/VatDesk.Infrastructure`,
14	  `src/VatDesk.Core`)
15	- Frontend: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite (`frontend/`), served as static files by the
16	  API in production — single deployable unit
17	- Database: PostgreSQL via EF Core + Npgsql; migrations apply automatically on startup
18	- Country model: pluggable per-country VAT registry + declaration strategy; Hungary
19	  (`HU`) is the only implemented country in v1
20	
21	## Quick start
22	
23	```bash
24	docker compose up --build
25	```

...(truncated)
```

</details>

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mariuscretu/Documents/repos/VATDesk/README.md",
  "old_string": "## Quick start\n\n```bash\ndocker compose up --build\n```\n\nThis starts PostgreSQL and the app (API + built frontend served together) at\n`http://localhost:8080`. `GET /api/health` reports app version and DB connectivity.\n\nFor frontend hot-reload during development, run the API and the Vite dev server\nseparately:\n\n```bash\ndotnet run --project src/VatDesk.Api\ncd frontend && npm install && npm run dev\n```\n\nThe Vite dev server proxies `/api/*` to the API (see `frontend/vite.config.ts`).\n\n<!-- TODO: document required env vars (ConnectionStrings__Default, Jwt__Key) once auth lands -->",
  "new_string": "## Quick start\n\n```bash\ncp .env.example .env\n# then edit .env and set JWT_KEY to a real generated value:\n#   openssl rand -base64 48\ndocker compose up --build\n```\n\nThis starts PostgreSQL and the app (API + built frontend served together) at\n`http://localhost:8080`. `GET /api/health` reports app version and DB connectivity.\n\n`.env` is required — there is no built-in fallback signing key (see\n[Security](#security)). The app fails fast at startup with a clear error if `JWT_KEY` is\nmissing or shorter than 32 bytes.\n\nFor frontend hot-reload during development, run the API and the Vite dev server\nseparately:\n\n```bash\ndotnet run --project src/VatDesk.Api\ncd frontend && npm install && npm run dev\n```\n\nThe Vite dev server proxies `/api/*` to the API (see `frontend/vite.config.ts`).\n\n### Environment variables\n\n| Variable | Required | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|\n| `JWT_KEY` | yes | HS256 signing key, ≥ 32 bytes. No default — generate with `openssl rand -base64 48`. |\n| `POSTGRES_USER` / `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` / `POSTGRES_DB` | no (demo defaults) | Local-only Postgres credentials for docker-compose; not internet-facing. |"
}
```

</details>

import type { UserRole } from '@/api/types'

const TOKEN_KEY = 'vatdesk-token'
const USER_KEY = 'vatdesk-user'

export interface StoredUser {
  userId: string
  email: string
  name: string
  role: UserRole
}

// Token lives primarily in memory (AuthContext React state); sessionStorage here is only
// a rehydration mechanism so a page refresh doesn't force a re-login. Tradeoff: anything
// reachable via sessionStorage is readable by an XSS payload, same as localStorage — an
// httpOnly cookie would be the safer pattern, but that needs server-issued cookies/CSRF
// handling this demo's scope doesn't build. sessionStorage (vs. localStorage) at least
// scopes the exposure to the tab's lifetime rather than persisting indefinitely.
export function getToken(): string | null {
  return sessionStorage.getItem(TOKEN_KEY)
}

export function getStoredUser(): StoredUser | null {
  const raw = sessionStorage.getItem(USER_KEY)
  if (!raw) return null
  try {
    return JSON.parse(raw) as StoredUser
...(truncated)
```

  "content": "# VATDesk — Security\n\nPhase 6 security audit and hardening pass against the 11-point checklist in\n`.claude/skills/hungarian-vat/references/architecture.md`. This document is the final\nstate after that session: audit → fix → prove, in that order (see `docs/PLAN.md` and the\nPhase 6 commit history for the full trail — every fix below is its own commit referencing\nthe checklist item number).\n\n## Checklist status\n\n| # | Item | Status | Evidence |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| 1 | Upload limits | **Implemented** | Three independent layers: Kestrel global `Limits.MaxRequestBodySize` ([Program.cs](../src/VatDesk.Api/Program.cs)), per-endpoint `[RequestSizeLimit]` on `DeclarationsController.Upload`, and `FormOptions.MultipartBodyLengthLimit` — all set to the same 5 MB (`ParserFactory.MaxFileSizeBytes`). Extension allow-list and content-sniffing both genuinely reject with 400, not just log (tested). |\n| 2 | XML / XXE | **Implemented** | `NavXmlInvoiceParser`: `DtdProcessing.Prohibit` + `XmlResolver = null` + `MaxCharactersInDocument = 5_000_000`, all in the one active parse path. Verified live: temporarily weakening `DtdProcessing.Prohibit` alone still left the XXE test passing (`XmlResolver = null` independently blocks it) — genuine defense-in-depth, not a single point of failure. |\n| 3 | Parsing (decimal / invariant culture) | **Implemented** | Zero `double`/`float` in `VatDesk.Core`/`VatDesk.Infrastructure` production code. `FieldParsing.cs` and `CsvInvoiceParser`'s `CsvConfiguration` both use `CultureInfo.InvariantCulture`. Frontend never re-parses amounts, only formats server-supplied numbers. |\n| 4 | AuthN (JWT) | **Implemented** | Key from env only (`Jwt__Key`), fail-fast at startup on missing/<32-byte key, 30-min expiry, HS256 pinned on both issuance (`SigningCredentials`) and validation (`ValidAlgorithms = [HmacSha256]`). `docker-compose.yml`'s committed fallback signin
...(truncated)
```

## Assistant <sup>7/21/2026, 5:36:15 PM</sup>

## Phase 6 complete

**Stage A** (audit) delivered the full verdict table before any code changed: 6 Implemented, 1 N/A, 4 Partial. **Stage B** fixed every gap, one focused commit per checklist item. **Stage C** proved each testable protection with tests that fail if the protection regresses, verified headers live via `docker compose` + `curl`, and wrote `docs/SECURITY.md`.

**What was actually broken and got fixed:**
- Kestrel's global body-size cap was never set (#1)
- `docker-compose.yml` committed a real, working JWT signing key as a fallback default (#4) — a genuine secret in git, not just a demo nicety
- JWT validation didn't pin `ValidAlgorithms` (#4)
- Upload had zero rate limiting (#6) — which surfaced a real bug: `UseRateLimiter` ran before `UseAuthentication`, so per-user partitioning would have silently degraded to per-IP
- No CORS policy, no security headers, no CSP existed at all (#7, #8) — fixing CSP required extracting the Vite build's one inline script so `script-src 'self'` wouldn't silently break the app
- HSTS would have been dead in the real Azure deployment (TLS terminates at the platform edge) without `UseForwardedHeaders` — caught during Stage C's manual verification, not assumed
- `GlobalExceptionHandler` was silently sending `application/json` instead of `application/problem+json` — caught by the Stage C test written specifically to prove #9

**What was already correct**, verified rather than trusted: upload size limits, XXE hardening (confirmed via a live mutation test), decimal/invariant-culture parsing, authorization on every endpoint, login rate limiting.

**Surfaced, not silently fixed**: the skill's own `architecture.md` API table is missing `/api/samples/*` and `/api/health` — flagged in `docs/SECURITY.md` rather than edited.

93/93 tests green, frontend builds clean, `docs/SECURITY.md` is the full reference, README's Security section links it, and `docs/PLAN.md` Phase 6 is checked off with a one-line-per-fix summary.


---

## User <sup>7/21/2026, 5:40:16 PM</sup>

Read CLAUDE.md and docs/PLAN.md. This session is a pure frontend refactor: 
replace all fetch-based API interaction with axios, centralized in a single 
client with interceptors. HARD RULE: zero behavior change observable by the 
user or by the backend, except the explicitly listed caching additions. No 
visual changes, no new features, no backend changes.

STAGE A — INVENTORY (no code changes)
Find every fetch call and every place that constructs headers, parses 
ProblemDetails, handles 401/403, uploads multipart, or downloads blobs. List 
them as a table: file | endpoint | method | special behavior (auth, blob, 
multipart, redirect-on-401, error mapping). This table is the migration 
checklist — every row must be ticked by the end.

STAGE B — Central axios client (frontend/src/api/client.ts)
- One axios instance: baseURL from the existing config, JSON defaults, sensible 
  timeout (e.g. 30s; uploads may override).
- Request interceptor: attach Authorization from the auth store (same source 
  4.1 uses — do not create a second token source).
- Response interceptor (error side):
  * Normalize errors into ONE typed shape (e.g. ApiError { status, title, 
    detail, problemDetails? }) parsed from ProblemDetails when present; 
    network/timeout errors map into the same shape with status 0.
  * 401 ⇒ exactly the existing behavior: clear session, redirect to /login 
    preserving intended destination. Guard against redirect loops (a 401 from 
    /api/auth/login itself must NOT trigger the redirect — it's an invalid 
    credentials case for the form).
  * 403 and other statuses: pass the normalized error through; pages keep 
    their existing handling.
- MIGRATION TRAP — handle explicitly: fetch resolves on 4xx/5xx and code 
  checked response.ok; axios throws. Every migrated call site must move its 
  non-2xx logling from "if (!ok)" branches into catch/error paths WITHOUT 
  changing what the user sees. Audit each row of the Stage A table for this 
  specifically.
- Special cases:
  * File upload (POST /api/declarations): multipart FormData — let axios set 
    the boundary (do not hard-code Content-Type), preserve the existing 
    progress UX; onUploadProgress may drive the existing presentational steps 
    but do not redesign them.
  * PDF download: responseType 'blob', preserve filename logic and the 
    button's loading/error states.
  * Sample file links: if they are plain <a href> downloads today, LEAVE them 
    as links — do not convert working native downloads into axios calls.

STAGE C — Caching (the only behavior addition)
- Introduce a minimal, explicit cache layer — hand-rolled Map with TTL or a 
  tiny helper, NOT a new heavyweight dependency (no TanStack Query in this 
  session; note it as a possible future improvement instead).
- Cache exactly these:
  * GET /api/countries/HU/vat-categories — effectively immutable: cache for 
    the session, shared across pages (report view re-renders must not refetch).
  * GET /api/auth/me — cache until logout/401.
- Explicit invalidation: full cache clear on logout and on 401 redirect.
- Do NOT cache: declarations list (4.4 will own freshness), individual 
  declaration fetches (cheap, and status could change in future), anything 
  non-GET.
- Document the cache policy in a comment block at the cache module top.

STAGE D — Migrate + verify
- Migrate every Stage A row to the client. Delete dead fetch helpers. No 
  component may import axios directly — only the client module (grep-enforced: 
  "from 'axios'" appears in client.ts only).
- Tick each inventory row with the file diff reference.
- TypeScript strict: zero errors; no `any` in the error normalization.

ACCEPTANCE — full manual regression in browser (docker compose up):
- Login: success path; wrong password shows the SAME inline error as before 
  (not a redirect); rate-limit response still surfaces sensibly.
- Session: refresh keeps login; logout clears; deep-link bounce and return 
  still work; expired/invalid token on any page ⇒ redirect to /login with 
  destination preserved.
- Upload: sample-clean.csv end-to-end ⇒ report shows the golden totals 
  (94 300 / 50 600 / 43 700); oversized file still rejected client-side with 
  no network call; spoofed .csv still shows the designed server-rejection 
  screen.
- Report: renders identically; PDF downloads with the same filename; viewer 
  role still sees no admin affordances; 404 state unchanged.
- Caching proof: with devtools network tab open, navigate between report pages 
  — vat-categories fetched exactly once per session; after logout+login it 
  refetches.
- git grep fetch( in frontend/src returns nothing API-related (allow unrelated 
  uses if any, list them).

CONSTRAINTS
- Pure refactor discipline: if you find a pre-existing bug, do NOT fix it 
  silently — list it under "Found during refactor" and leave behavior as-is 
  unless it's trivially safe; I'll decide.
- Conventional commits: inventory/infra, migration, caching as separate 
  commits. Final commit: note the refactor in docs/PLAN.md under a "Technical 
  improvements" line (do not disturb phase checkboxes), and end with the ticked 
  inventory table + any "Found during refactor" items.

# VATDesk — Project Plan

Hungarian VAT (ÁFA) declaration generator. This document is the agreed implementation plan; domain

| Topic | Decision |
|---|---|

| Frontend | React 18 + TypeScript + Vite |
| Database | PostgreSQL, EF Core + Npgsql, migrations auto-applied on startup |
| PDF | QuestPDF (Community license) |
| Packaging | Multi-stage Dockerfile; docker-compose (app + postgres); single deployable unit (API serves built React app) |
| Deployment | Azure (Web App for Containers / Container Apps); Railway as fallback |
| Input formats | CSV (primary) + NAV 3.0-flavored XML; format by content sniffing |
| Declaration output | v1 = summary totals per VAT category, split OUT/IN, with net VAT payable (NOT the official 2465A form line structure) |
| Persistence | Aggregates + validation issues only; raw invoice rows NOT stored (PII tradeoff, documented) |
...(truncated)

1	import { clearSession, getToken } from '@/lib/auth-storage'
2	import type {
3	  DeclarationDto,
4	  DeclarationListItemDto,
5	  HealthResponse,
6	  LoginRequestDto,
7	  LoginResponseDto,
8	  MeDto,
9	  VatCategoryDto,
10	} from './types'
11	
12	export type SampleFileName = 'clean.csv' | 'invalid.csv' | 'nav.xml'
13	
14	interface ProblemDetails {
15	  title?: string
16	  detail?: string
17	  status?: number
18	}
19	
20	export class ApiError extends Error {
21	  status: number
22	  detail?: string
23	
24	  constructor(status: number, problem?: ProblemDetails) {
25	    super(problem?.title ?? `Request failed (${status})`)
26	    this.status = status
27	    this.detail = problem?.detail
28	  }
29	}
30	
31	async function parseProblem(response: Response): Promise<ProblemDetails | undefined> {
32	  try {
33	    return (await response.json()) as ProblemDetails
34	  } catch {
35	    return undefined
36	  }

import type { UserRole } from '@/api/types'

const TOKEN_KEY = 'vatdesk-token'
const USER_KEY = 'vatdesk-user'

export interface StoredUser {
  userId: string
  email: string
  name: string
  role: UserRole
}

// Token lives primarily in memory (AuthContext React state); sessionStorage here is only
// a rehydration mechanism so a page refresh doesn't force a re-login. Tradeoff: anything
// reachable via sessionStorage is readable by an XSS payload, same as localStorage — an
// httpOnly cookie would be the safer pattern, but that needs server-issued cookies/CSRF
// handling this demo's scope doesn't build. sessionStorage (vs. localStorage) at least
// scopes the exposure to the tab's lifetime rather than persisting indefinitely.
export function getToken(): string | null {
  return sessionStorage.getItem(TOKEN_KEY)
}

export function getStoredUser(): StoredUser | null {
  const raw = sessionStorage.getItem(USER_KEY)
  if (!raw) return null
  try {
    return JSON.parse(raw) as StoredUser
...(truncated)
```

import type { ReactNode } from 'react'

import type { UserRole } from '@/api/types'
import { useAuth } from '@/context/AuthContext'

interface RequireRoleProps {
  role: UserRole | UserRole[]
  children: ReactNode
  fallback?: ReactNode
}

/**
 * Cosmetic role gate — hides UI the current user's role shouldn't see. The API's
 * [Authorize(Roles = ...)] policies are the real enforcement; this component only ever
 * saves a round trip / avoids showing a button that would 403. Never treat a hidden
 * button here as a substitute for the server check.
 */
export function RequireRole({ role, children, fallback = null }: RequireRoleProps) {
  const { user } = useAuth()
  const allowedRoles = Array.isArray(role) ? role : [role]

  if (!user || !allowedRoles.includes(user.role)) {
    return <>{fallback}</>
  }

  return <>{children}</>
}

I read `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/PLAN.md`, and traced every `fetch()` call and its callers. Full migration checklist:

| # | File(s) | Endpoint | Method | Special behavior |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `client.ts` (`loginRequest`) → `AuthContext.login` → `LoginPage` | `/api/auth/login` | POST | Raw `fetch`, separate from `apiFetch` — **no** auth header, **no** 401-redirect (a 401 here is "wrong password," an inline form error, not a session death). JSON body. |
| 2 | `client.ts` (`getMe`) | `/api/auth/me` | GET | Via `apiFetch` (auth header + 401-redirect). **Defined but never called anywhere in the UI** — dead code today. |
| 3 | `client.ts` (`getHealth`) | `/api/health` | GET | Via `apiFetch`. **Defined but never called anywhere in the UI** — dead code today. |
| 4 | `client.ts` (`getDeclarations`) → `DashboardPage` | `/api/declarations` | GET | Via `apiFetch`. Generic catch → `'error'` state (no status-specific handling). |
| 5 | `client.ts` (`getDeclaration`) → `DeclarationReportPage` | `/api/declarations/{id}` | GET | Via `apiFetch`, run in `Promise.all` with row 7. 404 → `'not-found'` state; anything else → `'error'` state. |
| 6 | `client.ts` (`uploadDeclaration`) → `UploadPage.process` | `/api/declarations` | POST | **Multipart** `FormData` — browser must set `Content-Type`/boundary, never hard-coded. **MIGRATION TRAP**: catch block branches on `err instanceof ApiError` to distinguish "server responded" (401 silent-return / 403 forbidden-screen / other → full-page error) from "network failure" (toast + Retry). Axios will make network failures *also* throw an `ApiError`-shaped object once normalized, so this branch must switch to checking `status === 0` instead of `instanceof ApiError`. |
| 7 | `client.ts` (`getVatCategories`) → `DeclarationReportPage` | `/api/countries/HU/vat-categories` | GET | Via `apiFetch`. **Already ad-hoc cached** (a `Map<string, Promise<...>>` keyed by country code, no TTL, no invalidation on logout) — Stage C replaces this with the real cache module. |
| 8 | `client.ts` (`fetchSampleBlob` → `fetchSampleAsFile`, `downloadSample`) → `DropZone`, `FormatHintTabs`, `UploadErrorScreen` | `/api/samples/{name}` | GET | Separate `authedBlobFetch` (duplicates the auth-header + 401-redirect logic from `apiFetch`). **Blob** response — not a plain `<a href>` link, it's an authenticated JS fetch that then synthesizes an anchor click to trigger save. Must migrate, not leave as a link. |
| 9 | `client.ts` (`downloadDeclarationPdf`) → `DeclarationReportPage`, `DeclarationsTable` | `/api/declarations/{id}/pdf` | GET | Same `authedBlobFetch`. Blob response, per-caller loading state + error toast, filename `declaration-{id.slice(0,8)}.pdf`. |

**Shared infrastructure to migrate**: `ApiError` class (status + detail), `parseProblem` (parses ProblemDetails JSON), the duplicated 401-redirect logic (appears twice — `apiFetch` and `authedBlobFetch`), `saveBlob` helper (unchanged — pure blob→disk, no fetch involved).

**Token source**: `getToken()`/`clearSession()` from `lib/auth-storage.ts` — this is the one source I'll wire the axios interceptor to; `AuthContext` doesn't hold a second copy.

**No `<a href>` static download links exist** — every "download" in this app is already an authenticated JS fetch, so row 8 doesn't get the "leave as link" exemption.

I'll move into Stage B now (central axios client), then Stage C (caching), then Stage D (migrate every row above + delete the dead `fetch` helpers), ticking this table off as I go.

(status === 0 ? ProblemDetails is\n  parsed correctly even for blob-responseType requests (samples, PDF), where\n  axios pre-parses the error body as a Blob instead of JSON — read out\n  explicitly via Blob.text() + JSON.parse.\n- 401 handling is unchanged: clear session, redirect to /login with the\n  intended destination preserved, except a 401 from POST /api/auth/login\n  itself (checked by request URL), which stays an inline form error.\n\nMigration trap handled: UploadPage.process's catch block used to branch on\n\"err instanceof ApiError\" to tell a server error apart from a network\nfailure (fetch resolves on 4xx/5xx; only a true network failure was some\nother thrown error). Axios throws on every non-2xx AND now normalizes\nnetwork failures into the same ApiError shape, so a network failure would\nhave silently fallen into the wrong branch (the full-page rejection screen\ninstead of the toast+Retry). Fixed by checking `status !== 0` instead of\n`instanceof ApiError` — same two outcomes, correct branch for each.\n\nNo component imports axios directly — only client.ts does (grep-verified).\nSample-file \"downloads\" were already authenticated JS fetches (not native\n<a href> links), so they migrate like every other blob endpoint.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude

{
  "file_path": "/Users/mariuscretu/Documents/repos/VATDesk/frontend/src/api/cache.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Cache policy\n * ------------\n * A minimal, explicit, in-memory cache — a plain Map, no library, no TTL. TTL isn't\n * needed because invalidation here is purely event-driven, not time-based:\n *\n * - GET /api/countries/HU/vat-categories: effectively immutable reference data. Cached\n *   for the lifetime of the session, shared across every page that needs it, so\n *   navigating between report views never refetches it.\n * - GET /api/auth/me: cached until logout or a 401. The authenticated user's identity\n *   doesn't change mid-session.\n * - Nothing else goes through this cache: the declarations list (Phase 4.4 owns its own\n *   freshness), individual declaration fetches (cheap, and status could change), and no\n *   non-GET request is ever a candidate.\n *\n * Invalidation is exactly two triggers, both explicit, both full-clear (not per-key):\n * logout (AuthContext.logout) and a 401 response (api/client.ts's response interceptor).\n * A failed request is never cached — see `cached()` below.\n *\n * Not TanStack Query or any other data-fetching library — deliberately out of scope for\n * this session (noted as a possible future improvement instead of adopted here).\n */\n\nconst cache = new Map<string, Promise<unknown>>()\n\n/** Returns the cached promise for `key` if one exists, otherwise calls `fetcher`, caches its promise, and returns that. A rejected fetch is evicted immediately so the next call retries instead of replaying the same failure. */\nexport function cached<T>(key: string, fetcher: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {\n  const existing = cache.get(key)\n  if (existing) {\n    return existing as Promise<T>\n  }\n\n  const promise = fetcher().catch((error: unknown) => {\n    cache.delete(key)\n    throw error\n  })\n  cache.set(key, promise)\n  return promise\n}\n\n/** Full clear — called on logout and on a 401 redirect. Never partial/per-ke
...(truncated)

{
  "command": "git add frontend/src/api/cache.ts frontend/src/api/client.ts frontend/src/context/AuthContext.tsx && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfeat(frontend): add explicit session-scoped cache for vat-categories and me\n\nThe only behavior addition in this session's axios refactor. A minimal\nhand-rolled Map-based cache (api/cache.ts, no TanStack Query — noted as a\npossible future improvement instead), documented with its exact policy in a\ncomment block at the module top:\n\n- GET /api/countries/HU/vat-categories: cached for the session (replaces the\n  ad-hoc per-country Map that already lived in client.ts, now centralized).\n- GET /api/auth/me: newly cached until logout/401 (the function existed\n  already but was never actually called from the UI — caching it now is\n  forward-looking, not a behavior change to anything currently running).\n- Everything else — declarations list, individual declarations, all blob\n  downloads — stays uncached, unchanged.\n\nInvalidation is explicit and total: clearApiCache() runs on logout\n(AuthContext.logout) and on every 401 (client.ts's response interceptor,\nright where the session is already being cleared).\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\""
}
```

9	|---|---|
10	| Backend | ASP.NET Core 8 (LTS) Web API |
11	| Frontend | React 18 + TypeScript + Vite |
12	| Database | PostgreSQL, EF Core + Npgsql, migrations auto-applied on startup |
13	| PDF | QuestPDF (Community license) |
14	| Packaging | Multi-stage Dockerfile; docker-compose (app + postgres); single deployable unit (API serves built React app) |
15	| Deployment | Azure (Web App for Containers / Container Apps); Railway as fallback |
16	| Input formats | CSV (primary) + NAV 3.0-flavored XML; format by content sniffing |
17	| Declaration output | v1 = summary totals per VAT category, split OUT/IN, with net VAT payable (NOT the official 2465A form line structure) |
18	| Persistence | Aggregates + validation issues only; raw invoice
...(truncated)
```

## Assistant <sup>7/21/2026, 6:09:17 PM</sup>

## Done — axios migration complete, zero observable behavior change

**5 commits**, each independently reviewable:
1. `chore`: add axios dependency
2. `refactor`: migrate every `fetch()` to the central axios client — including the migration-trap fix in `UploadPage.tsx` (network failures now normalize into `ApiError{status:0}` instead of a different error type, so the catch block had to switch from `instanceof ApiError` to `status !== 0` to keep the same toast-vs-full-screen-error behavior)
3. `feat`: the one explicit behavior addition — session-scoped caching for vat-categories and `/api/auth/me`, invalidated on logout/401
4. `docs`: PLAN.md technical-improvements record

**Two real bugs my own test script hit, both false alarms in the actual code** — worth knowing about since they briefly looked like regressions:
- Testing cache behavior via `page.goto()` trivially "resets" any in-memory cache (fresh JS module load) — not a bug, just a wrong test technique. Switched to real client-side navigation, which then correctly showed 0 refetches across 3 navigations and exactly 1 refetch after logout+login.
- Logging out from the report page redirects back to that page after login (the existing deep-link-preservation feature), not to `/` — correct pre-existing behavior, not something I introduced.

**Full manual regression passed** in docker compose: wrong password → inline error (not redirect); refresh/logout/deep-link-bounce-and-return all intact; oversized file rejected client-side with zero network calls; sample-clean.csv → exact golden totals (94 300/50 600/43 700), unchanged report screenshot; PDF downloads with the same filename convention; spoofed .csv still shows the designed rejection screen; viewer sees no admin affordances; 404 state unchanged; login rate-limiting still trips sensibly. `git grep "fetch(" frontend/src` → nothing; axios imported only in `client.ts`.

Backend untouched — 93/93 xUnit tests green throughout, confirming this really was frontend-only.

**Found during refactor, left as-is** (per instructions, your call): `getMe()`/`getHealth()` are fully implemented but never called from any page (dead code predating this session); `FormatHintTabs`/`UploadErrorScreen`'s download handlers have no `catch`, so a failed sample download is an unhandled promise rejection today.
